method_atom: A form-factor technique from exactly-integrable field theory used to derive the exact large-distance asymptotics (leading and subleading terms) of two-point correlation functions of a many-body system. The central mechanism is a non-commutativity of two limiting procedures: taking the long-range asymptotic limit and taking a weak-coupling limit of a control parameter do not commute, because in one class of observables the leading and subleading asymptotic terms interfere as the parameter approaches its limit, producing a discontinuity relative to the naive weak-coupling (linearized) approximation.
object_atom: A two-dimensional classical Coulomb gas of pointlike positive and negative unit charges interacting through a logarithmic potential (equivalent to an integrable sine-Gordon field model), studied in thermal equilibrium in the high-temperature Debye-Hückel regime, specifically its charge and number-density correlation functions.
brief_reason: The form-factor asymptotic analysis and the discovery that the long-distance limit and the weak-coupling (high-temperature) limit fail to commute is the transferable machinery, while the two-dimensional gas of logarithmically interacting +/- charges is the physics-specific object.
